setting.note: Choukri's Tangier is the hungry underside of the international city: the Grand Socco and Petit Socco markets, the alleys of the medina, the working port, and the cafés and back rooms of the interzone. The map runs from the famine-struck Rif countryside near Nador, which drives the family west to the coast, through the streets where the boy survives, out to a spell in Tetouan.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Mohamed Choukri, Al-Khubz al-Hafi, first written 1973. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: For Bread Alone, Mohamed Choukri (1973). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The rust-red of the Rif's dry earth and Tangier's ochre walls. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
